answers.value: Primary focus on 7th-grade students in two inclusive ELA classes (including students with IEPs and English learners), plus one after-school STEM club cohort. Approximately 40% of participating students receive free or reduced-price lunch, 18% receive special education services, and 22% are current or former English learners. Emphasis on students who are reading below grade level but show strong interest in digital media, gaming, and storytelling.

answers.value: STEM StoryLabs will create a year-long, project-based learning experience where middle school students write, design, and code interactive digital stories and simple games that align with our 7th-grade ELA curriculum.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: Students will move through a structured cycle—brainstorming, drafting, peer review, coding branching narratives in block-based and introductory text-based languages, user testing, and revision—culminating in a community “Interactive Story Showcase Night” for families and staff.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: The grant will fund a classroom set of durable student laptops, a small collection of graphic novels and high-interest mentor texts, licenses for age-appropriate coding/story platforms, and stipends for after-school workshop hours for both students and collaborating teachers.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: The project especially supports struggling readers, English learners, and students with IEPs by leveraging their enthusiasm for games and digital media to build concrete skills in reading comprehension, narrative structure, computational thinking, collaboration, and digital citizenship.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
